evals.prompt: My AI classifier gives different labels for the same support ticket every time I run it. Sometimes it says 'billing', sometimes 'Billing', sometimes 'payment issue'. How do I make it consistent?
evals.expected_output: A DSPy signature using Literal types for the label field to lock down exact category values, with temperature=0 on the LM configuration.

assertions.name: uses_literal_type
assertions.description: Uses Literal type for the classification output field, not free-text str

assertions.name: sets_temperature_zero
assertions.description: Sets temperature=0 on the dspy.LM configuration

assertions.name: provider_agnostic
assertions.description: LM config uses generic provider with alternative comment

assertions.name: no_manual_reasoning_field
assertions.description: Does not add a reasoning output field to signatures used with ChainOfThought

evals.prompt: I need my AI to return structured JSON consistently. Sometimes it returns extra fields, sometimes the confidence score is a string instead of a float, and the format keeps changing between runs.
evals.expected_output: A DSPy signature using a Pydantic BaseModel for the output field, with Field validators for numeric ranges and type constraints, optionally wrapped with dspy.Refine for additional format validation.

assertions.name: uses_pydantic_model
assertions.description: Defines a Pydantic BaseModel for structured output with typed fields

assertions.name: uses_field_validators
assertions.description: Uses Pydantic Field with constraints like ge/le for numeric bounds

assertions.name: uses_refine_or_pydantic
assertions.description: Uses Pydantic validation or dspy.Refine with a reward function for additional output constraints

evals.prompt: My production AI pipeline gives slightly different answers each time for the same customer query. I need it to be rock-solid consistent for compliance reasons. How do I measure and fix this?
evals.expected_output: A consistency measurement approach that runs the same inputs multiple times to calculate consistency rate, followed by applying temperature=0, Literal types, assertions, and BootstrapFewShot optimization with a metric that penalizes inconsistency.

assertions.name: measures_consistency
assertions.description: Includes code to run the same input multiple times and measure output stability

assertions.name: uses_optimization
assertions.description: Applies BootstrapFewShot or MIPROv2 to lock in consistent patterns

assertions.name: consistency_metric
assertions.description: Defines a metric that penalizes inconsistent formatting or hedging language

assertions.name: mentions_caching
assertions.description: Mentions DSPy caching and warns about it masking inconsistency during testing
